justification: All pass criteria met: 10 Given/When/Then criteria covering happy path (AC-1, AC-2), shipped/delivered edge case (AC-5, AC-6), already-cancelled idempotency (AC-7), plus authorization, courier notification, race condition, and refund failure scenarios. Multiple ambiguities surfaced in both confidence.low_confidence_areas (6 items) and escalations (4 detailed items) covering cancellable states, time windows, who can cancel, fee structure, multi-parcel, and coupon handling. Confidence level is 45, well below the 65 threshold. No invented rules presented as facts — all policy assumptions (BR-3 through BR-5) are explicitly marked invariant:false with justification noting they are policy decisions, and escalations explicitly flag them for PM validation.

observations.text: 10 acceptance criteria far exceed the minimum 3, covering happy path, terminal states, already-cancelled, authorization, courier notification, and refund failure
6 low_confidence_areas and 4 detailed escalations with options and recommendations — well above the 2 ambiguity minimum
Confidence level 45 correctly reflects the underspecified single-sentence input
Every non-invariant business rule is explicitly flagged as a policy assumption needing validation, not presented as fact
8 edge cases covering race conditions, idempotency, multi-parcel, fraud holds, and promotional discounts
Explicitly calls out the missing question of who can cancel (BR-1 assumes order owner only, escalation flags the broader policy)
BR-8 idempotency design for already-cancelled orders is a sophisticated domain insight
Cancellation window question surfaced as escalation option C in the first ambiguity item
